issues.description: The implementation does not appear to handle the positional TASK-ID argument for single-task emission or the specific exit code requirements for unknown IDs.
issues.suggestion: Implement CLI argument parsing to allow a single TASK-ID to be passed, and ensure it exits with code 1 and a stderr message if the ID is not found.

issues.description: Missing logic for emitting 'null' for missing frontmatter fields and graceful degradation for malformed frontmatter (parse_error: true).
issues.suggestion: Update the parsing loop to ensure all schema keys are present (using null if missing) and wrap parsing in a try-except block to catch malformed YAML, emitting the task with 'parse_error: true' instead of crashing.

issues.description: The current test suite is insufficient. It only covers 3 basic scenarios, whereas AC-008 requires coverage for empty projects, nested folders, malformed frontmatter, single-task lookup, and byte-stability.
issues.suggestion: Expand tests/unit/commands/test_task_status_json.py to include all scenarios listed in AC-008.

criteria_verification.notes: File exists with main() and __name__ guard.
JSON schema and sorting are implemented.
No evidence of null-filling for missing fields.
No evidence of graceful degradation for malformed YAML.
No evidence of single-task lookup via positional argument.
Tests passed do not cover the full breadth of required scenarios.
Cannot verify full test suite pass as the suite is incomplete.
No evidence of lint/format check results.

rationale: The core functionality is present, but the implementation fails to address critical error-handling, edge-case, and CLI argument requirements specified in the acceptance criteria. Additionally, the test coverage is significantly below the required threshold.
